contents.value: **def** — Elixir keyword.
**defp** — Elixir keyword.
**defmodule** — Elixir keyword.
**defmacro** — Elixir keyword.
**defmacrop** — Elixir keyword.
**IO.puts** — Built-in function/command in Elixir.
**IO.inspect** — Built-in function/command in Elixir.
**Enum.map** — Built-in function/command in Elixir.
**Enum.filter** — Built-in function/command in Elixir.
**Enum.reduce** — Built-in function/command in Elixir.
A **function** is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task.
A **variable** stores a value that can be referenced and modified.
A **loop** repeats a block of code while a condition is true or for each item in a collection.
A **conditional** executes different code branches based on a boolean test.
A **string** is a sequence of characters used for text.
An **array** (or list) is an ordered collection of elements.
A **dictionary** (hash/map) stores key-value pairs for fast lookup.
A **class** defines a blueprint for creating objects with attributes and methods.
A **module** is a file containing reusable code that can be imported.
An **exception** signals an error condition that can be caught and handled.
A **lambda** is a concise anonymous function expression.
A **closure** captures variables from its enclosing scope.
An **iterator** yields values one at a time from a sequence.
A **generator** lazily produces values using yield.
A **decorator** wraps a function to extend its behavior.

```elixir
defmodule Enum
```
The Enum module provides a comprehensive set of algorithms for enumerating over collections that implement the Enumerable protocol (lists, maps, ranges, streams). It includes functions for mapping, filtering, reducing, sorting, grouping, and chunking — forming the backbone of data transformation in Elixir.

```elixir
defmodule Map
```
The Map module provides functions for creating and manipulating maps, which are key-value stores in Elixir. Maps allow any value as a key, provide O(log n) access, and are the primary data structure for representing structured data. Map literals use the %{key => value} syntax.

```elixir
defmodule List
```
The List module provides functions specific to linked lists, which are the fundamental ordered collection in Elixir. Lists support pattern matching with [head | tail] destructuring, are immutable, and prepending is O(1). For general collection operations, use Enum instead.

```elixir
defmodule String
```
The String module provides functions for manipulating UTF-8 encoded binary strings. Elixir strings are binaries and fully support Unicode. The module handles grapheme clusters correctly, distinguishing between bytes, codepoints, and graphemes for accurate text processing.

```elixir
defmodule IO
```
The IO module provides functions for handling input and output operations in Elixir. It supports reading from and writing to standard I/O, files, and other I/O devices. IO functions accept iolists (nested lists of integers and binaries) for efficient output without prior concatenation.

```elixir
defmodule GenServer (behaviour)
```
A behaviour module for implementing client-server relationships in OTP. GenServer abstracts the common patterns of a server process that maintains state, handles synchronous (call) and asynchronous (cast) requests, and manages lifecycle callbacks (init, terminate, handle_info).

```elixir
defmodule Task
```
The Task module provides conveniences for spawning and awaiting concurrent work in Elixir. Tasks are used for running computations in parallel, especially for fan-out operations where multiple independent pieces of work can execute simultaneously and their results collected later.

```elixir
defmodule Stream
```
The Stream module provides lazy, composable operations on enumerables. Unlike Enum, Stream operations return lazy enumerables that are only computed when consumed. This enables processing large datasets or infinite sequences without loading everything into memory at once.

```elixir
defmodule File
```
Contains functions to manipulate files.

```elixir
defmodule Supervisor
```
A behaviour module for implementing supervisors. A supervisor is a process which supervises other processes, called child processes.

```elixir
defmodule Process
```
Conveniences for working with processes and the process dictionary.

```elixir
Number
```

Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```elixir

```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.


Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```elixir
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```elixir
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```elixir
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```elixir
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
